title: Nd metal successfully produced with fluoride-based feedstock without hazardous chemicals
description: CMI researchers from Ames National Laboratory and an industry partner conducted the activity for this highlight
text:
CMI researchers from Ames National Laboratory and an industry partner conducted the activity for this highlight
Innovation
Neodymium metal was produced by calciothermic reduction of water-free REE-fluoride made without using or generating hydrogen fluoride (HF).
Achievement
Produced Na-Nd-F without using HF, via a highly scalable synthesis method. Produced more than 120 g of Nd metal from calciothermic reduction of the Na-Nd-F.
Significance and Impact
Produced rare earth metals from feedstock bypassing use or generation of hazardous chemicals.
Support domestic production of Nd metal and Nd-Fe-B magnet.
